machinery_genes.quote: These genes encode homologs of the conserved core components TssB, TssC, Hcp (also known as TssD), TssE, TssF, TssG, TssM, TssH (also known as ClpV), TssA, TssK, and TssL.
machinery_genes.note: Named as a conserved core component of the AB307-0294 T6SS locus (18 genes, ABBFA_002240 to ABBFA_002223) in Fitzsimons et al 2018.

machinery_genes.quote: These genes encode homologs of the conserved core components TssB, TssC, Hcp (also known as TssD), TssE, TssF, TssG, TssM, TssH (also known as ClpV), TssA, TssK, and TssL.
machinery_genes.note: TssB shown to be required for Hcp secretion / functional T6SS in A. baumannii by Carruthers et al 2013 (strain M2); named as core component of the AB307-0294 locus by Fitzsimons et al 2018.

machinery_genes.quote: These genes encode homologs of the conserved core components TssB, TssC, Hcp (also known as TssD), TssE, TssF, TssG, TssM, TssH (also known as ClpV), TssA, TssK, and TssL.
machinery_genes.note: Hcp = TssD, the secreted inner tube. Counted as machinery per the T6SS brief. Carruthers et al 2013 demonstrated TssB-dependent Hcp secretion in A. baumannii (strain M2), confirming a functional T6SS.

machinery_genes.quote: Bioinformatic analysis of the AB307-0294 genome revealed three genes (ABBFA_002469, ABBFA_002276, and ABBFA_000116) that encoded proteins with high identity to VgrG (TssI) proteins...these genes were named vgrG1 (ABBFA_002469), vgrG2 (ABBFA_002276), and vgrG3 (ABBFA_000116)
machinery_genes.note: VgrG = TssI spike; counted as machinery per the T6SS brief. AB307-0294 encodes three VgrG proteins, each delivering one cargo effector (Tse15/Tde16/Tae17). locus_tag ABBFA_002469.

machinery_genes.quote: Bioinformatic analysis of the AB307-0294 genome revealed three genes (ABBFA_002469, ABBFA_002276, and ABBFA_000116) that encoded proteins with high identity to VgrG (TssI) proteins...these genes were named vgrG1 (ABBFA_002469), vgrG2 (ABBFA_002276), and vgrG3 (ABBFA_000116)
machinery_genes.note: VgrG = TssI spike; counted as machinery. locus_tag ABBFA_002276.

machinery_genes.quote: Bioinformatic analysis of the AB307-0294 genome revealed three genes (ABBFA_002469, ABBFA_002276, and ABBFA_000116) that encoded proteins with high identity to VgrG (TssI) proteins...these genes were named vgrG1 (ABBFA_002469), vgrG2 (ABBFA_002276), and vgrG3 (ABBFA_000116)
machinery_genes.note: VgrG = TssI spike; counted as machinery. locus_tag ABBFA_000116.

machinery_genes.quote: These included a number of known T6SS proteins...and two PAAR domain-containing proteins, one encoded within the main T6SS locus (ABBFA_002229) and one encoded elsewhere on the genome (ABBFA_002520).
machinery_genes.note: PAAR is secreted spike-tip machinery per the T6SS brief. The locus-internal PAAR protein (ABBFA_002229) is the one belonging to this T6SS instance; a second PAAR-domain protein (ABBFA_002520) is encoded elsewhere. Listed once as machinery PAAR (in-locus tag ABBFA_002229).

machinery_genes.quote: Also present in the A. baumannii T6SS locus are two T6SS-associated genes (tagF and tagN).
machinery_genes.note: T6SS-associated accessory gene within the AB307-0294 locus.

machinery_genes.quote: Also present in the A. baumannii T6SS locus are two T6SS-associated genes (tagF and tagN).
machinery_genes.note: T6SS-associated accessory gene within the AB307-0294 locus.

notes: AB307-0294 is the archetypal A. baumannii T6SS instance characterized by the Boyce/Harper (Monash) group. The complete core apparatus is named gene-by-gene in Fitzsimons et al 2018 (Infect Immun), which states the AB307-0294 T6SS locus contains 18 genes (ABBFA_002240 to ABBFA_002223) encoding the conserved core components TssB, TssC, Hcp/TssD, TssE, TssF, TssG, TssM, TssH/ClpV, TssA, TssK, and TssL, plus accessory tagF and tagN, plus an in-locus PAAR protein (ABBFA_002229). Three VgrG (TssI) spike proteins (vgrG1 ABBFA_002469, vgrG2 ABBFA_002276, vgrG3 ABBFA_000116) each carry one cargo effector. Carruthers et al 2013 independently demonstrated a functional A. baumannii T6SS (strain M2): TssB-dependent secretion of Hcp and tssB/hcp-dependent killing of E. coli (transferable apparatus confirmation for the same gene set). NOTE: this instance's RefSeq genome is NZ_CP012003; the Fitzsimons/Carruthers locus tags use the ABBFA_ prefix from the original AB307-0294 assembly (RefSeq NC_011595 / GenBank CP001172). Locus-tag-to-coordinate resolution against NZ_CP012003 is a later scripted step and was NOT performed here. EXCLUDED as non-machinery: the delivered cargo toxin effectors Tse15, Tde16, Tae17 (one is this instance's curated effector A1S_1292) and their immunity proteins; TssJ is explicitly ABSENT from the A. baumannii locus (the paper notes the locus lacks a TssJ homolog) so it is not listed. The second, genome-dispersed PAAR protein (ABBFA_002520) is not attributed to the main locus and is recorded only in the in-locus PAAR note.
